opportunities.title: Reverse AOV Decline via Combo Bundling & Upsell Strategy
opportunities.description: AOV dropped from ₹300.06 to ₹290.76 (-3.1%) despite 6.79% order growth. The average items per order also slightly declined from 1.818 to 1.802. With 2,782 orders/month, each ₹10 increase in AOV adds ₹27,820/month. Introducing combo meals (burger + fries + drink) and training staff on upselling high-margin sides (French Fries at ₹89, Mango Lassi at ₹76) can recover and exceed prior AOV levels. Beverages and sides currently account for only ~13% of revenue — well below the 20-25% industry norm.

evidence.value: 1.818 → 1.802 items/order
French Fries (4.1%) + Mango Lassi (4.0%) + Cold Coffee (2.0%) + others = ~13.1% of total revenue

assumptions: Min: Recover half the lost AOV (₹3/order × 2,782 orders = ₹83,460/month)
Expected: Add ₹6/order through combos (₹6 × 2,782 = ₹1,66,920/month)
Max: Achieve ₹10/order uplift bringing AOV back to prior level (₹10 × 2,782 = ₹2,78,200/month)
All values in paise as per system
actionable_steps: Create 3-4 combo meals: Smoky Chicken Burger + French Fries + Cold Coffee at 5-8% discount vs à la carte
Implement POS prompt for suggestive selling: 'Add Mango Lassi for ₹76?' on every main course order
Train takeaway counter staff on 'Would you like fries with that?' upsell script
Add combo pricing to menu boards and online ordering interface

opportunities.title: Grow Delivery Channel from 5.8% to 15% Revenue Share
opportunities.description: Delivery accounts for only 5.8% of revenue (₹4.72L from 165 orders) vs takeaway at 70.6%. Delivery AOV (₹28,634) is comparable to takeaway (₹29,174), showing demand is healthy but volume is extremely low. Delivery actually declined slightly from ₹4.84L (153 orders, AOV ₹31,643) — fewer orders but the prior period had higher AOV. Delivery represents a massive growth lever: even reaching 10% share (matching industry norms) would add ₹3.4L/month without new kitchen capacity.

evidence.value: ₹4,724,604 paise (₹4.72L) = 5.84% of total revenue, only 165 orders
₹4,841,228 paise (153 orders, AOV ₹31,643) — revenue declined 2.4% despite order growth
Off-peak hours (11AM-1PM, 9PM-10PM) have 3-4x fewer orders than peak, suggesting kitchen can absorb delivery volume

assumptions: Min: Add 80 delivery orders/month at AOV ₹290 = ₹2.3L/month
Expected: Grow to ~285 delivery orders (10% share) at AOV ₹290 = +120 orders × ₹290 = ₹3.4L/month
Max: Grow to ~415 delivery orders (15% share) = +250 orders × ₹290 = ₹7.4L/month
Assumes aggregator platform commission of ~25% not yet deducted — net impact is 75% of stated values
actionable_steps: List on 2-3 food delivery platforms (Swiggy, Zomato, etc.) if not already present
Create delivery-optimized menu: Focus on top 10 items that travel well (burgers, wraps, rice bowls)
Offer first-week delivery promotions (10% off first delivery order)
Schedule delivery-focused promotions during off-peak hours (11AM-1PM) to utilize idle kitchen capacity
Optimize packaging for delivery to maintain food quality

opportunities.title: Capture Weekend Revenue Uplift with Premium Weekend Menu
opportunities.description: A dramatic behavioral shift occurred: Last period weekends had LOWER AOV than weekdays (-3.6%), but this period weekends now show +7.6% PREMIUM over weekdays (₹307 vs ₹285). Weekend orders also grew from 684 to 705 (+3.1%). This indicates customers are willing to spend more on weekends. However, weekend revenue is still only 26.8% of total — despite weekends being 28.6% of days. There's an opportunity to create premium weekend-only offerings to amplify this premium behavior.

evidence.value: Previous: weekday ₹302.94 vs weekend ₹291.96 (-3.6%). Current: weekday ₹285.26 vs weekend ₹306.98 (+7.6%)
₹351.81 AOV at 7PM weekends (122 orders) — highest regular slot, 21% above overall AOV
₹397.31 AOV at 10PM weekends (11 orders) — premium late-night opportunity

assumptions: Min: Add ₹15/order AOV on 705 weekend orders = ₹1.05L/month
Expected: Add ₹30/order on weekends through premium items = ₹2.11L/month
Max: Add ₹30/order AND grow weekend orders by 50% = ₹4.22L/month
10PM weekend slot has small sample (n=11) — premium signal needs validation
actionable_steps: Launch 'Weekend Specials' menu with 3-4 premium items (₹350-450 range) available Sat-Sun only
Extend weekend operating hours to capture 10PM premium demand (currently very low volume)
Create weekend family combo meals targeting higher basket sizes
Promote weekend specials via social media/SMS to regulars by Thursday each week

opportunities.title: Accelerate Growth of Grilled Fish Wrap — Highest Revenue Per Order
opportunities.description: Grilled Fish Wrap has the highest revenue per order at ₹311.59 (vs overall AOV of ₹290.76) and grew 17.7% in revenue (₹4.40L → ₹5.17L) with quantity jumping from 164 to 193 (+17.7%). It's the 5th highest revenue item but has low order penetration (166/2782 = 6.0%). Its high per-order revenue suggests customers buying it also buy sides/drinks. This is a premium item with strong momentum that deserves promotional investment.

evidence.value: ₹311.59 revenue/order — 7.2% above average AOV
Only 6.0% of orders include this item vs Masala Dosa at 28.1% and Smoky Chicken Burger at 18.3%

assumptions: Min: Grow penetration from 6.0% to 7.0% (+28 orders × ₹268 = ₹0.52L/month incremental revenue from item alone)
Expected: Grow to 8.5% (+70 orders) with halo effect on AOV = ₹1.04L/month
Max: Grow to 11% penetration (+140 orders) = ₹2.07L/month
Assumes current price of ₹268 maintained
actionable_steps: Feature Grilled Fish Wrap as 'Chef's Recommendation' on menu boards and POS screens
Create a premium combo: Grilled Fish Wrap + Masala Lemonade at ₹310 (vs ₹332 à la carte)
Add prominent food photography for this item on takeaway counter and delivery platforms
Test a 'Fish Friday' promotion to build weekly habit around this item

opportunities.title: Optimize Off-Peak Hours to Increase Daily Order Throughput
opportunities.description: There's a massive volume disparity between peak and off-peak hours. The 7PM hour generates 474 orders while 11AM generates only 54 total (42 weekday + 12 weekend). The lunch ramp-up from 11AM-1PM and the late night 9-10PM windows are significantly underutilized. Shifting even 5% of capacity to off-peak via targeted promotions could add substantial incremental revenue without operational strain.

evidence.value: 7PM: 474 orders (₹14.56L). 11AM: 54 orders (₹1.44L). Ratio = 8.8:1
9PM: 143 orders (₹4.10L), 10PM: 57 orders (₹1.79L) — sharp drop from 8PM's 360 orders
Only 12 orders on weekends at 11AM (AOV ₹179, lowest on entire grid) vs 42 weekday orders

assumptions: Min: Add 28 orders/month in off-peak slots at AOV ₹290 = ₹0.8L/month
Expected: Add 55 orders/month via happy hour and early bird promos = ₹1.6L/month
Max: Add 111 orders/month (4% of current base) = ₹3.2L/month
Assumes kitchen has capacity during off-peak (very likely given low volume)
actionable_steps: Launch 'Early Bird Lunch' 11AM-12PM with 10% discount on combos
Create 'Late Night Bites' menu (9-10PM) featuring quick-prep items like French Fries, Rolls, Cold Coffee
Offer time-limited delivery discounts during 11AM-1PM on delivery platforms
Weekend brunch menu (11AM-12PM) to activate the weakest slot

risks.title: Extreme Channel Concentration: 70.6% Revenue from Takeaway
risks.description: Takeaway generates ₹57.09L (70.6% of revenue) from 1,957 orders. This increased from ₹54.38L (69.6%) last period, meaning concentration is GROWING. Dine-in is flat at ~₹19L and delivery is marginal at ₹4.7L. Any disruption to takeaway (new competitor, road construction, parking issues, weather) could devastate revenue. The business effectively has a single-channel dependency.

evidence.value: 70.6% of revenue (₹57,094,206 paise) from takeaway, up from 69.6% prior period
Only 5.84% of revenue from delivery (165 orders) — no diversification occurring

mitigation_steps: Aggressively grow delivery to 15%+ of revenue (see OPP-002)
Invest in dine-in experience improvements to grow that channel (ambiance, seating, service speed)
Build direct ordering capability (WhatsApp/website) to reduce platform dependency
Diversify takeaway customer base through loyalty programs and new catchment marketing

risks.title: Declining AOV Despite Growing Orders — Revenue Efficiency Erosion
risks.description: AOV fell 3.1% from ₹300.06 to ₹290.76 while order volume grew 6.79%. Revenue grew only 3.49% — meaning the business is working harder (more orders, more kitchen load, more packaging) for diminishing marginal returns. If AOV continues declining at this rate while order growth plateaus, revenue could stagnate or decline. Items per order also declined (1.818 → 1.802), confirming customers are ordering less per visit.

evidence.metric: Revenue growth gap vs order growth
evidence.value: Revenue +3.49% vs Orders +6.79% — 3.3 percentage point gap indicating AOV drag

mitigation_steps: Implement combo/bundle pricing immediately (see OPP-001) to arrest AOV decline
Review if any recent price reductions or discount promotions are driving down AOV
Analyze whether new customer acquisition is bringing in lower-spending segments
Set AOV targets for staff and introduce suggestive selling KPIs
Monitor weekly AOV trends to detect if decline is accelerating

risks.title: Top 3 Menu Items Drive 46.8% of Revenue — Menu Concentration Risk
risks.description: Smoky Chicken Burger (17.9%), Masala Dosa (15.0%), and Chicken Rice Bowl (13.9%) together generate 46.8% of all revenue. This concentration has remained stable (46.7% last period), meaning the business hasn't diversified. Supply disruption, ingredient price spikes, or changing consumer preferences for any of these three items could significantly impact revenue. Additionally, 2 of the top 3 are chicken-based, creating protein-specific supply chain risk.

evidence.value: 46.8% of revenue from 3 items (current) vs 46.7% (prior period) — no improvement
Smoky Chicken Burger (₹14.23L) + Chicken Rice Bowl (₹11.08L) = ₹25.31L = 31.8% of revenue depends on chicken supply

mitigation_steps: Promote mid-tier items like Grilled Fish Wrap and Paneer Tikka Wrap to distribute revenue
Develop 2-3 new menu items in the ₹200-250 range to fill gaps in the menu
Secure backup chicken supplier to mitigate single-protein supply risk
Run quarterly limited-time offers to test new items and diversify customer preferences
Set target to reduce top-3 concentration below 40% within 3 months
risks.timeline: 2-4 weeks for promotions, 1-3 months for new item development

risks.title: No Inventory, Supplier, or Kitchen Performance Data — Blind Spots
risks.description: Critical operational systems appear to have no data: supplier spend tracking returned zero records, inventory/waste tracking returned zero records, and kitchen performance metrics returned zero. This means the restaurant is operating without visibility into food costs, waste levels, supplier reliability, or kitchen efficiency. Without this data, it's impossible to manage food cost percentage (industry benchmark: 30%) or identify operational bottlenecks.

evidence.value: 0 supplier records, 0 total spend tracked
0 waste events, 0 inventory value tracked
0 kitchen time records, 0% completion rate tracked

mitigation_steps: Implement inventory management system immediately — even a basic spreadsheet tracking daily purchases
Begin recording food cost for top 5 items to establish baseline food cost percentage
Set up kitchen order tracking (ticket timestamps) to measure prep times
Conduct a one-time waste audit over 7 days to establish waste baseline
At ₹80.9L monthly revenue, even 2% untracked waste = ₹1.6L/month lost
risks.timeline: Immediate — start basic tracking within 1 week, full system within 4 weeks

insights.executive_summary: The restaurant generated ₹80.89L in revenue over 30 days (2,782 orders), growing 3.49% vs the prior period driven by a 6.79% increase in order volume. However, **Average Order Value declined 3.1%** (₹300 → ₹291), meaning growth is coming from volume rather than value — an unsustainable pattern. **Takeaway dominates at 70.6%** of revenue with delivery severely underdeveloped at 5.8%. The top 3 menu items drive 46.8% of revenue with 2 of 3 being chicken-based. **Five key opportunities** identified: (1) Reverse AOV decline through combo bundling (+₹1.7L/month expected), (2) Grow delivery channel to 10%+ share (+₹3.4L/month), (3) Capitalize on emerging weekend premium behavior (+₹2.1L/month), (4) Promote high-value Grilled Fish Wrap (+₹1.0L/month), and (5) Activate off-peak hours (+₹1.6L/month). Total opportunity: **₹9.8L/month expected**. **Four critical risks** require attention: channel over-concentration on takeaway, declining AOV trajectory, menu concentration on 3 items, and complete absence of cost/inventory/kitchen tracking data — the last being the most urgent operational gap. **Immediate priorities:** (1) Implement combo/upsell strategy within 1 week to arrest AOV decline, (2) Begin basic cost tracking to understand true profitability, (3) Start delivery channel expansion within 2 weeks.
